title: API HTTP Package Topology Implementation Tasks
principles: Preserve HTTP API runtime behaviour while moving the implemented package to the long-term topology.
Keep apps/api as the only standalone API process/runtime owner.
Keep packages/api/http focused on HTTP transport contracts, route layers, handler adapters, OpenAPI and typed HTTP clients.
Keep calculator behaviour in @taxkit/calculators and SDK facades in @taxkit/sdk.
Prefer direct import/package updates over compatibility aliases because @taxkit/http-api is still private.
Verify final call graphs against source imports, package metadata and docs before acceptance.
beforeImplementation: Create an active execution plan under docs/exec-plans/active/ before editing runtime or package metadata.
Record current references to @taxkit/http-api and packages/http-api before moving files.
Implement one task at a time with parent review before starting the next task.
Do not run bun run version-repo unless the user explicitly asks for release versioning.

globalVerification.commitPolicy: Commit after each coherent implementation slice passes mandatory verification, Changeset review and parent audit.
requiredBeforeFinalPR: bun run changeset or explicit no-changeset rationale for docs-only slices
evidenceRequired: Current and final call graph summary for apps/api, apps/web, @taxkit/api-http and @taxkit/sdk
Old-reference audit with documented historical exceptions
Browser-safe import audit for apps/web and docs/browser modules
SDK independence audit proving no @taxkit/api-http dependency in @taxkit/sdk
Changeset path and release-train impact, or explicit no-changeset rationale
Verification command outcomes and residual risks

tasks.title: Relocate the HTTP API package and update runtime imports
tasks.goal: Make packages/api/http and @taxkit/api-http the implemented HTTP API package without changing route behaviour.
scope: Add the nested workspace glob needed for packages/api/http.
Update dependency declarations, source imports, test imports, Knip/Turbo/package config and the lockfile.
Preserve the current export map shape under the new package name.
Keep apps/api as the runtime host and apps/web as an HTTP client.
outputs: Any package/build config files that reference the old path or package name

mandatorySubagentContract: You are implementing exactly one task from the TaxKit spec/task list.
Before editing, read the target spec, this task object, relevant current files, and the relevant docs in docs/architecture/*.
Identify the owning package for every schema, type, ID, service, layer and tagged error you touch.
Review the spec's call-graph diagrams before editing, and update or report them if implementation discovers a different final graph.
MUST use Effect-native primitives when they fit: Data, Schema, Array, Chunk, HashSet, HashMap, Match, Context, Layer, Config, Service, Record, Result, Exit, Bun, Platform, Command and ManagedRuntime.

Do not fall back to plain TypeScript control flow, nullable values, mutable records, mutable arrays, switch, Object.values, Object.entries, ad hoc _tag objects, unsafe casts, stringly branching, custom env parsing or hand-rolled runtime wrappers when an Effect/schema/platform primitive fits.
Reuse canonical schema-derived types, branded IDs, tagged errors, service contracts and constructors from the owning package.
Do not create mirrored DTOs, local duplicate types, local duplicate schemas or transport-only shape copies when an owning schema or API/SDK contract already exists.

Keep one-off Effect error handling and transformations inline at the callsite. Do not extract tiny mapper/wrapper helpers for single-use Effect.mapError, Effect.catchTag, Effect.catchAll or Effect.catchAllDefect.
Browser/runtime code must consume browser-safe API/SDK exports instead of importing server-only internals.
Package-facing changes MUST add or update a Changeset with bun run changeset, using a user-facing changelog summary. If the task is not package-facing, state that explicitly in the handoff.
Run this task's mandatory verification gates, including bun run verification unless the task explicitly documents a narrower gate.
Run task-specific tests, smoke checks, browser checks or architecture audits required by the task's blast radius.
For substantial code, API, SDK, app, package-boundary or docs-runtime work, run and document at least three implementation improvement audit passes before handoff.
Audit the diff for helper sprawl, canonical type/schema/id/error reuse, unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, stringly branching and browser-safe imports where relevant.
Report the Changeset path and release-train impact, or report why no Changeset was required.
Report changed files, verification commands, outcomes and residual risks.
Report whether the final implementation still matches the spec's call graph.
Do not start or delegate another task. The parent agent must review, audit, verify and explicitly accept this task before the next task begins.
taskSpecificScope: Relocate the implemented package only. Do not redesign handlers, route schemas, calculator logic, SDK facades or API endpoint paths.
Update current runtime/source imports from @taxkit/http-api to @taxkit/api-http.
Preserve browser-safe imports in apps/web and server-only imports in apps/api and HTTP API tests.
Do not leave a compatibility package at packages/http-api unless a concrete blocker is documented and accepted by the parent.

mandatoryVerification: bun install --lockfile-only or equivalent lockfile refresh if workspace metadata changes the lockfile
rg audit over apps packages package.json knip.json turbo.json proving current runtime/source/config imports no longer use @taxkit/http-api or packages/http-api
browserVerification: No browser-visible route behaviour is expected to change.
Audit apps/web imports so browser/runtime code uses @taxkit/api-http/client or @taxkit/api-http/client/live only.
If a browser route or rendered UI changes unexpectedly, run the focused browser or route smoke check before handoff.
qualityAudits: Audit pass 1: final production and test call graphs still match the spec after the package move.

Audit pass 2: package boundaries remain intact: apps/api owns runtime startup, @taxkit/api-http owns transport, @taxkit/calculators owns calculator orchestration and @taxkit/sdk has no HTTP API dependency.
Audit pass 3: Effect-native control flow, canonical schema/type/error reuse, no unsafe casts, no local DTO mirrors, no helper sprawl and no browser/server import leaks.
completionCriteria: packages/http-api no longer contains the implemented package.
apps/api imports @taxkit/api-http/server and still owns Bun process startup.
apps/web imports only browser-safe @taxkit/api-http client exports.
@taxkit/sdk source and package metadata do not depend on @taxkit/api-http.
The package-local test, typecheck and build gates pass under the new package name.
A Changeset exists for the package-facing rename and relocation.
Parent agent reviewed the diff, verification evidence, Changeset and final call graph before accepting the task.

tasks.title: Refresh current docs and public references
tasks.goal: Make current documentation, public docs and package READMEs describe @taxkit/api-http as the implemented package while preserving historical context.
scope: Update root maps, architecture docs, package/app READMEs and public MDX docs that describe the current HTTP API package.
Update docs validation or navigation references affected by the moved path.
Preserve old @taxkit/http-api mentions only in historical specs, completed execution plans and changelogs where the context is explicitly historical.
Update the active execution plan with docs/reference audit evidence when implementation begins.

taskSpecificScope: This task is docs/reference alignment after the package move.
Do not broaden into generated docs inventory, endpoint redesign or app UI work.
If only docs are touched after the package-facing Changeset from API-HTTP-001, either update that Changeset before commit or record an explicit no-new-changeset rationale.

mandatoryVerification: rg audit proving current docs and public MDX use @taxkit/api-http and packages/api/http for the implemented package
rg audit listing any remaining @taxkit/http-api or packages/http-api mentions and classifying them as historical, changelog or completed-plan context
bun run changeset or explicit no-new-changeset rationale tied to the package-facing Changeset from API-HTTP-001
browserVerification: No browser UI behaviour is expected to change.
If public docs content or route examples change visible output, run the focused docs route/browser check or capture browser evidence before handoff.
qualityAudits: Audit pass 1: docs describe current ownership accurately and link to architecture docs instead of duplicating package manuals.
Audit pass 2: public docs stay focused on open-source TaxKit API, SDK and docs surfaces and do not import private reference-repo details.
Audit pass 3: historical mentions of @taxkit/http-api remain only where useful and are not presented as current package guidance.
completionCriteria: Current root, architecture, app, package and public docs point to packages/api/http and @taxkit/api-http.
The documentation audit no longer lists packages/api/http as planned while the package is implemented.
Historical references to packages/http-api or @taxkit/http-api are either updated or explicitly historical.
Parent agent reviewed docs diffs against the spec and architecture docs before accepting the task.

tasks.title: Complete compatibility, API smoke and release-train audit
tasks.goal: Prove the relocated package is stable across package filters, API app runtime, docs, SDK boundaries and release metadata.
scope: Run full repo/package verification after code and docs alignment.
Run or record local API smoke evidence for the unchanged public routes.
Review Changeset package naming and release-train impact.
Update the active execution plan with final evidence and residual risks.
Do not version the repo or publish packages.
outputs: Any final docs/spec corrections required by verification
Any final package metadata corrections required by verification

taskSpecificScope: This is final validation and correction work only.
Run the broad gates, audit the final graph, and make only corrections required to satisfy the spec.
Do not run bun run version-repo, remove private flags, publish packages, or start unrelated cleanup.

mandatoryVerification: Local API smoke checks for GET /api/health, GET /api/v1/calculators, POST /api/v1/calculators/:calculatorId/calculate and GET /api/docs/openapi.json, or a documented environment blocker with package-level in-process test evidence
browserVerification: Run a focused apps/web smoke check if route runtime imports changed beyond package-name updates.
Browser/runtime code must not import @taxkit/api-http/server, @taxkit/api-http/client/server or handler subpaths.
qualityAudits: Audit pass 1: full source/import graph matches the target production and test call graphs.
Audit pass 2: package metadata, workspaces, lockfile, filters, Changeset and changelog naming are coherent.
Audit pass 3: no residual old-name current guidance, browser/server import leaks, SDK HTTP API dependency, unsafe casts, DTO mirrors or helper sprawl were introduced.
completionCriteria: All required final verification gates pass or any blocker is recorded for replan/user decision.
API smoke evidence confirms public route behaviour stayed stable, or the environment blocker is documented with in-process test evidence.
Changeset package name and release-train impact match the spec.
The active execution plan records final validation evidence.
Parent agent completes final diff review and accepts the rollout before commit/push/PR work.
